page: The Vultr vx1-m-192c-1536gb is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It is equipped with an AMD processor configured with 192 vCPUs and 192 physical cores, operating with a single thread per core. The system features 1435.0 GB of system memory, which translates to approximately 7.47 GB of RAM per core.

This instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model, meaning compute resources are shared with other tenants on the physical host. It does not feature local storage, instead provisioning 1 GB of network-attached storage, and does not include any graphics processing units. Network provisioning includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given its shared CPU architecture and minimal network-attached storage, this server offers a qualitative cost-efficiency profile suited for memory-bound tasks rather than sustained high-performance compute or intensive local disk I/O. It is best suited for workloads such as large-scale caching, in-memory databases, and data processing tasks that require a massive memory footprint but can tolerate shared CPU scheduling.
description: The Vultr vx1-m-192c-1536gb is an x86_64 server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring an AMD processor with 192 vCPUs and 192 physical cores. It provides 1435.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.47 GB of RAM per core. Operating on a shared CPU allocation model, this instance shares host compute resources with other tenants. It lacks local storage, utilizing 1 GB of network-attached storage, and contains no GPUs. The server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. This configuration offers high memory density, making it suitable for memory-intensive applications, large caching layers, and in-memory databases that do not require dedicated CPU cores.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-192c-1536gb is an x86_64 server with 192 AMD vCPUs, 1435 GB of RAM, and a shared CPU allocation model, designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring minimal local storage.

tagline: A high-density memory instance featuring shared AMD compute cores and network storage for memory-intensive workloads.

1435.0 GB of system memory providing 7.47 GB per core
Shared CPU allocation model for cost-efficient resource density
